document: RBI Payment Data Localisation Requirement (India - Row 17)

sections.heading: It is observed that not all system providers store the payments data in India. In order to ensure
sections.title: Payment data storage requirement for system providers
sections.summary: System providers must store all payment system data within India, including end-to-end transaction details. Foreign transaction data may be stored abroad if necessary. System providers must comply with this requirement within six months and report compliance to the Reserve Bank by October 15, 2018.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires payment data to be stored within India, creating a data residency requirement that impacts cross-border data transfers and storage arrangements for payment systems.

requirements.text: All system providers shall ensure that the entire data relating to payment systems operated by them are stored in a system only in India
For the foreign leg of the transaction, if any, the data can also be stored in the foreign country, if required
System providers shall ensure compliance of the data storage requirement within a period of six months
System providers shall report compliance to the Reserve Bank latest by October 15, 2018

sections.source_quote: It is observed that not all system providers store the payments data in India. In order to ensure better monitoring, it is important to have unfettered supervisory access to data stored with these system providers as also with their service providers / intermediaries/ third party vendors and other entities in the payment ecosystem.
sections.relevance_reason: Core financial data storage and cross-border transfer rules

sections.heading: 3. The directive is issued under Section 10(2) read with Section 18 of Payment and Settlement
sections.title: Legal basis for the directive
sections.summary: This section states that the directive is issued under Section 10(2) read with Section 18 of the Payment and Settlement Systems Act 2007.
sections.source_quote: The directive is issued under Section 10(2) read with Section 18 of Payment and Settlement Systems Act 2007, (Act 51 of 2007).
